InvalidPhone: Please enter a valid 10-digit phone number.
RagiInfoText: Finger millet (Ragi) is the staple crop of Southern Karnataka. Highly resilient to drought, it requires minimal water and is packed with vital nutrients like calcium, iron, and fiber. It serves as a major agricultural crop in districts like Anekal, Devanahalli, Doddaballapur, Nelamangala, and Hosakote.
HeroSubtitle: Empowering Karnataka farmers with precision soil science.
RagiAdvisorySubtitle: A practical Karnataka regional baseline, with no AI guesses and no automatic pesticide spraying.

No automatic pesticide spray: No automatic pesticide spray is recommended. Select crop stage and symptoms with a local officer for any chemical decision.
warning_regional_baseline: Regional baseline, not soil-test specific. Scan a Soil Health Card and verify N, P and K for soil-specific guidance.
warning_stcr_disabled: STCR target-yield equations are disabled until locally validated coefficients are approved.
warning_verified_categories: Verified soil categories: N {{n}}, P {{p}}, K {{k}}. Category-rule guidance must be locally reviewed before farmer release.
timing_dap_rainfed: Basal at sowing; band/place away from seed.
timing_dap_irrigated: Basal at sowing; band/place away from seed.
timing_urea_rainfed: Split only with adequate soil moisture; do not apply to dry stressed crop.
timing_urea_irrigated: Split: half basal, half at 25–30 DAS after irrigation.
timing_mop: Apply basal and incorporate into soil.
